title: CONCLUSIONS
abstract_summary: This study investigates optimal compositions and heat treatment effects in Fe-Mn-Al austenitic alloys (Fermanal type) for lightweight, high-strength applications. Over 100 heats with Mn 5-45%, Al 2-14%, and C 0.05-1.5% were quenched from 1150°C and aged at 400-700°C. Microstructure, tensile properties, hardness, magnetic permeability, oxidation resistance, and density were characterized, revealing optimal aging near 500°C and a strong dependence of properties on Al content.
research_objective: To determine the optimal compositions of Fe-Mn-Al alloys and the effect of heat treatment on their structure, mechanical, and physicochemical properties.
material_systems: Nominal composition ranges: Mn 5-45%, Al 2-14%, C 0.05-1.5%
Phases: γ-solid solution, α-solid solution, carbides, Mn-type brittle phase
main_process_variables: Melting: laboratory induction furnace, helium atmosphere, investment casting
Tensile samples: 5 mm diameter, 25 mm gauge length

key_sections.role: Background of Fermanal alloys and research objective
Alloy preparation, heat treatment schedules, and testing methods
Phase diagram at 1150°C, effect of carbon on phase structure, aging microstructure evolution, mechanical properties vs. aging temperature, magnetic permeability, oxidation resistance, density
Summary of optimal compositions and property trends
